questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A measures 50° and angle B measures 70°. What is the measure of angle C?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. So angle C = 180° − 50° − 70° = 60°.

questions.question: A right triangle has one acute angle of 35°. What is the measure of the other acute angle?
questions.explanation: In a right triangle, the two acute angles sum to 90°. The other acute angle = 90° − 35° = 55°.

questions.question: In an isosceles triangle, the vertex angle (between the two equal sides) is 40°. What is the measure of each base angle?
questions.explanation: The two base angles of an isosceles triangle are equal. If the vertex angle is 40°, then each base angle = (180° − 40°) ÷ 2 = 70°.

questions.question: Two angles of a triangle measure 25° and 115°. Is this a valid triangle? Explain in one sentence.
questions.answer: No, the third angle would be 40°, which is valid, but check: 25 + 115 + 40 = 180. Actually, yes, it is valid.
questions.explanation: The third angle = 180° − 25° − 115° = 40°. All three angles are positive and sum to 180°, so it is a valid triangle.

questions.question: In triangle XYZ, the exterior angle at vertex Z is 120°. What is the measure of the interior angle at Z?
questions.explanation: An interior angle and its exterior angle are supplementary (sum to 180°). Interior angle Z = 180° − 120° = 60°.

questions.question: A triangle has sides of length 5, 12, and 13. Without calculating, explain why this could be a right triangle.
questions.explanation: Check if the two smaller sides squared sum to the largest side squared: 5² + 12² = 25 + 144 = 169 = 13². This is a Pythagorean triple, so it is a right triangle.
